create_enchantment_industry.ponder.blaze: Automating with Mechanical Arms
Blaze Enchanters can be automated with Mechanical Arms
Mechanical Arms can insert items for enchanting
Mechanical Arms can also feed Blaze Enchanters experience
Mechanical Arms can extract enchanted and invalid items
Introduction to Blaze Enchanters
This is a Blaze Enchanter, which functions like an Enchanting Table
Since golden armor has higher enchantability than diamond armor, it will usually produce higher-level enchantments
However, higher enchanting levels are not always better. Some enchantments won't appear when the enchanting level is too high!
Provide it Liquid Experience to activate it
Before enchanting with a Blaze Enchanter, the enchanting level must be set via the panel. The level cap of a vanilla enchanting table is 30
The panel is also a filter slot. Right click that slot with an item to designate that item as a template. This state is called Template Enchanting Mode
In Template Enchanting Mode, Blaze Enchanters can enchant Enchanting Templates
Template Enchanting Mode filters enchantments to only those compatible with the template item
For example, placing a chestplate in the filter slot causes only chestplate enchantments to be generated
Giving a Blaze Enchanter a blank Enchanting Template will enchant it using the aforementioned filter
Different materials and template items will produce different enchantments
Super Enchanting with Blaze Enchanters
The Blaze Enchanter has two "stomachs". Feed it a Cake o' Enchanting...
...and it will begin seething. This state is known as Super Enchanting mode
While in Super Enchanting mode, the enchanting level cap is significantly increased. Additionally, treasure enchantments can now be produced
Blaze Enchanters in Super Enchanting mode exclusively process Super Enchanting Templates
Make sure to place a Lightning Rod nearby
Super Enchanting can cause lightning strikes
You can cover the Blaze Enchanter with blocks to avoid lightning strikes, but then curses may appear
If no Lightning Rods are present...
Automating with Mechanical Arms
Blaze Forgers can be automated with Mechanical Arms
Mechanical Arms can insert items for forging
Mechanical Arms can also feed Blaze Forgers experience
Mechanical Arms can extract forged items
Introduction to Blaze Forgers
This is a Blaze Forger, which functions like an anvil
Provide it Liquid Experience to activate it
Just like anvils, Blaze Forgers can merge enchantments of the same item, except there's no repair cost
Blaze Forgers can also merge Enchanting Templates
Most importantly, Blaze Forgers can apply Enchanting Templates to items!
Also, Blaze Forgers can strip enchantments from equipment, books, or Enchanting Templates onto blank Enchanting Templates!
Super Enchanting with Blaze Forgers
The Blaze Forger has two "stomachs". Feed it a Cake o' Enchanting...
...and it will begin seething. This state is known as Super Enchanting mode
While merging enchantments in Super Enchanting mode, Blaze Forgers can surpass the vanilla enchantment level cap and merge conflicting enchantments together
Blaze Forgers in Super Enchanting mode exclusively process Super Enchanting Templates
Make sure to place a Lightning Rod nearby
Super Enchanting can cause lightning strikes
You can cover the Blaze Forger with blocks to avoid lightning strikes, but this may introduce repair cost
If no Lightning Rods are present...
create_enchantment_industry.ponder.exper: Things you might want to know
There are a few more things you need to know before you're ready to start using Enchantment Industry
Blocks of Experience are no longer purely decorative and storage blocks. You'll need them later
Blocks of Experience are required to make both Enchanting Templates and Blocks of Super Experience
This is a Block of Super Experience, used to make Super Enchanting Templates
Blaze Enchanter
Blaze Forger
Seething Blaze Forgers and Enchanters are in Super Enchanting mode
Super Enchanting allows you to surpass traditional enchanting limits
Specifically, this includes exceeding the vanilla enchantment level cap, merging conflicting enchantments, and obtaining treasure enchantments directly
As Beacon base block
Introduction to Experience Handling
This is a tank of Liquid Experience, the primary form of Experience used in Enchantment Industry
If you need to refill a container item with Liquid Experience, use a Spout
Liquid Experience leaked from pipes will turn into experience orbs
Converting the experience you carry into liquid form requires two components
Liquid Hatch for accessing liquids directly from items
Experience Hatch for accessing experience directly from the player
But as part of Create, automation is a must, right?
You can use Item Drains to extract Liquid Experience from items
Mechanical Grindstones can also pulverize items. For example, they can convert Experience Nuggets into Liquid Experience
Crushing Wheels now have a chance of dropping a very small amount of Nuggets of Experience when killing creatures
Similarly, Deployers now drop Nuggets of Experience when killing creatures
Prepare materials for Super Enchanting
First, obtain a Block of Super Experience, which will be used to make a Super Enchanting Template. This requires a Block of Experience
Next, place a Lightning Rod
Lighting strike!
Natural lightning strikes aren't guaranteed to transform Blocks of Experience; only lightning strikes caused by Super Enchanting are guaranteed to transform them
Second, obtain a Cake o' Enchanting. It works as "Super Experience" and causes Blaze Forgers and Enchanters to enter Super Enchanting mode.
If Cakes o' Enchanting are used to put Blaze Forgers and Enchanters into Super Enchanting mode, what are Blocks of Super Experience used for?
Answer: crafting Super Enchanting Templates
In Super Enchanting mode, Blaze Forgers and Enchanters won't accept normal Enchanting Templates; they must use Super Enchanting Templates
Introduction to Experience Hatches
The Experience Hatch is simple to use. Right click it to store experience...
...or Shift-Right-Click it to extract stored experience
On the underside of the Hatch, there is a filter slot and a scroll panel. Using the panel, you can configure how much experience is stored or extracted per interaction
The filter slot is used to deal with experience fluids from other mods
For example, assume that Liquid Cyan Dye is experience fluid from another mod
Place a Bucket of Cyan Dye in the filter slot
You can now directly insert and extract Cyan Dye as "Cyan Experience"
Introduction to Experience Lanterns
Experience Lanterns absorb experience from nearby players and experience orbs. They glow relative to the amount of experience stored inside them
Experience Lantern also works on contraptions
create_enchantment_industry.ponder.mecha: Mechanical Grindstones not only have all the features of a grindstone...
...but also all the features of Sand Paper
Items can be manually applied to Mechanical Grindstones
Right-click an Item Drain with a Mechanical Grindstone...
This is a Grindstone Drain. Place another Mechanical Grindstone on top to use it
Experience in item form can be crushed into liquid form with Mechanical Grindstones
create_enchantment_industry.ponder.print: Before use, set the item to print via the filter slot...
Printers can also rename items, copy train schedules, copy clipboards, change package addresses, and more. Use JEI to look up printing recipes
